bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me the lowest value on every issue, so there is no useful concession signal yet; I will counter with a strong bid using only valid issues and values.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.02, and the opponent has not yet shown any concession beyond repeating an all-valueC offer. To avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA bid while making only a small concession, I am yielding on a low-weight issue and holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not conceded from their all-valueC position, which is very poor for me. To avoid simply repeating my last offer, I am rotating the small concession from issueD to another low-weight issue while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A, B, and C.

I will keep my highest-weight issues A, B, and C at valueA while rotating a concession on a low-weight issue to avoid simply repeating my prior bid.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while showing some movement to avoid stalemate. Their latest bid improves issueC to valueB, but they remain very far from my preferred values, so I will concede mainly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.80, which is an early-game concession from my prior offers while still preserving my highest-weight issues A, B, and C at my preferred valueA. The opponent has repeatedly pushed valueC but has recently conceded to valueB on issueA and issueC, so I read some movement but not enough to justify giving up high-importance issues at t=0.16. At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility around 0.76 while making concessions mainly on my lower-weight issues.
